title: Parallel SMT Batch Processing
initialization_flow: AbstractRecordProcessor.initialize() stores recordService and transformations as fields
activity_flow: LOG: debug — thread submitting N records for processing
FORK (parallel): for each SourceRecord in records (forEachOrdered)
PARALLEL BRANCH: recordService.submit(new TransformRecord(record, transformations)) → Future<SourceRecord>
JOIN: collect all Future<SourceRecord> into recordFutures list
SEQUENTIAL LOOP: for each Future<SourceRecord> f in recordFutures
WAIT: f.get() — blocks until transform completes (no timeout)
key_relationships: ParallelSmtBatchProcessor depends_on ExecutorService (recordService) for parallel task submission
ParallelSmtBatchProcessor depends_on Transformations (SMT chain) via TransformRecord callable
ParallelSmtBatchProcessor depends_on DebeziumEngine.ChangeConsumer (userHandler) for batch delivery
ParallelSmtBatchProcessor depends_on DebeziumEngine.RecordCommitter (committer) passed to handleBatch

concurrency_model.submission: forEachOrdered — preserves record order when submitting futures
concurrency_model.execution: recordService (ExecutorService / thread pool) runs TransformRecord callables in parallel
concurrency_model.collection: sequential f.get() per future — waits indefinitely for each; full batch gathered before handler call
concurrency_model.null_filtering: Records filtered to null by SMT chain are dropped before batch is handed to userHandler

processing_callables.TransformRecord: Callable<SourceRecord> — applies transformations.transform(record), returns null if filtered
processing_callables.TransformAndConsumeRecord: Callable<Void> — transform then push to Consumer<SourceRecord> (used by other processors)
processing_callables.TransformAndConvertRecord<R>: Callable<R> — transform then convert via Function<SourceRecord,R> (used by other processors)
processing_callables.TransformConvertConsumeRecord<R>: Callable<Void> — transform, convert, consume (used by other processors)

important_notes: ParallelSmtBatchProcessor is specifically for ChangeConsumer users who want parallel SMT but no format conversion — records stay as SourceRecord.
Ordering is maintained via forEachOrdered during submission; futures are collected in submission order.
f.get() has no timeout — processing will block indefinitely waiting for each record's transformation to complete.
Null records (filtered by SMT chain) are silently dropped from the batch before userHandler.handleBatch() is called.
committer is passed through to userHandler — offset commit responsibility lies with the user-provided ChangeConsumer.
ProcessingCallables.TransformRecord is the callable used exclusively by this processor (not TransformAndConsumeRecord or others).
